summary: To determine if azoospermia stems from testicular problems or blocked tubes, perform Testicular Fine-Needle Aspiration Cytopathology; positive findings indicate viable testicles capable of producing sperm, while negatives point to internal production failures even when hormone levels appear sufficient. This helps guide further treatment options like ICSI directly using extracted healthy gametes regardless of current ductal status. Agent1 reasoned by explaining how specific tests can help identify causes behind low/no sperm count based primarily on anatomical vs physiological origins, focusing clearly on actionable diagnostics crucially linked back to patient care pathways post-diagnosis confirmation.
